changes_made: Changed k8s_audit_logging_enabled to k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled for consistency with resource naming
review_notes: The functions generally map well to the CCCS CM-9 control, focusing on configuration management aspects like audit logging and encryption. However, the naming of 'k8s_audit_logging_enabled' was adjusted to 'k8s_apiserver_audit_logging_enabled' to align with the naming conventions and resource specificity. All functions are technically accurate and relevant to Kubernetes security controls.
naming_improvements: Use 'apiserver' as the resource name for audit logging functions to maintain consistency with other API server related checks.
title: CCCS CM-9: Configuration Management Plan
step1_reasoning: The CCCS CM-9 control focuses on configuration management, which can be mapped to ensuring that Kubernetes audit logging is enabled and configured to track changes. Additionally, ensuring that secrets and etcd data are encrypted at rest aligns with secure configuration management practices. These checks ensure that configuration changes are logged and sensitive data is protected, which are key aspects of a configuration management plan.
